title: Hieracium eriosphaera
taxonomy: Hieracium eriosphaera is classified within the family Asteraceae. This plant is a species of hawkweed, belonging to the genus Hieracium. The genus Hieracium is a group of flowering plants that are typically found in temperate regions. Within the Asteraceae family, Hieracium eriosphaera is further classified as a member of the subfamily Cichorioideae. The species Hieracium eriosphaera is native to New Zealand.

intro: Hieracium eriosphaera, also known as hawkweed, is a perennial plant species belonging to the dandelion family (Asteraceae) and is native to the mountainous regions of Europe. The plant has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, offering potential health benefits such as anti-inflammatory and antioxidant effects, which can aid in reducing inflammation and protecting against cell damage. The bioactive constituents of Hieracium eriosphaera include sesquiterpene lactones, flavonoids, and phenolic acids, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. The plant is commonly used to prepare herbal teas, tinctures, and infusions, which are often used to support digestive health and to reduce inflammation in the body.
